summary: Android back handling is still not wired into the PublicMenu state machine, and the cart-local info modal cannot participate in any page-level back stack.

findings.title: In-page views and overlays still bypass browser back entirely
findings.description: PM-S81-15 remains unfixed in the reviewed code. `view`, `drawerMode`, `selectedDishId`, `reviewDialogOpen`, and `isHelpModalOpen` are all controlled only via React state (`setView`, `setDrawerMode`, `setSelectedDishId`, etc.), but there is no matching `window.history.pushState()` on open and no `popstate` listener to unwind them. As a result, Android Chrome/WebView back will still leave `/x` from cart, checkout, confirmation/order-status, help, and review overlays instead of closing the topmost UI layer and returning to the menu.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single back-stack layer manager in `x.jsx`: push a synthetic history entry whenever a drawer/modal/sub-view opens, listen for `popstate`, and close the highest-priority active layer (`selectedDishId`/review/help modal -> drawer -> `view !== "menu"`) before allowing normal navigation.

findings.title: CartView info modal is invisible to any page-level back handler
findings.description: `CartView` owns its own `infoModal` state (`'online' | 'tableCode'`) and renders the overlay internally, with open/close actions entirely inside the drawer component. Even if `x.jsx` starts handling Back for the cart drawer, pressing Back while this modal is open will not close it first, because the parent page has no visibility into `infoModal`. That misses the task requirement that an open modal should consume Back before the drawer/page does.
findings.suggested_fix: Lift `infoModal` state to `x.jsx`, or let `CartView` register/unregister its own synthetic history entry and `popstate` cleanup when the modal opens so Back closes the modal before the drawer.

missing_tests: Android/WebView back from each in-page state (`cart`, `checkout`, `confirmation`, `orderstatus`, help modal, review dialog, dish reviews modal) should unwind UI state before leaving `/x`.
Back while `CartView`'s `infoModal` is open should close the modal first, then close the drawer on the next Back.
After synthetic history entries are consumed, Back from the menu root should still perform normal browser/app exit navigation.
notes: The bundle's target section contains `CartView.jsx`; I additionally inspected `pages/PublicMenu/base/x.jsx` because the task and bundle bug references for PM-S81-15 point there.
I did not find any existing `pushState`/`popstate` handling in the reviewed code.
